title: Monthly Content and Campaign Planning
summary: A monthly planning system that turns strategy into a realistic content calendar and launch sequence for the next operating cycle.
inputs: Business priorities for the next month
Product, sales, and customer feedback inputs

steps.title: Translate business priorities into marketing themes
steps.description: Start from the commercial priorities for the month and decide which topics, offers, or launches marketing must support.

steps.title: Map the calendar
steps.description: Lay out the month by week, including campaign dates, supporting content, channel distribution, and review points.

steps.title: Assign owners and dependencies
steps.description: Document who owns copy, creative, approvals, landing pages, and measurement setup for each planned item.

steps.title: Stress-test capacity
steps.description: Remove or simplify work that the team is unlikely to ship well. Keep the calendar realistic enough to execute cleanly.

whenToImplement: Use this once marketing activity is frequent enough that ad hoc planning is causing missed launches, inconsistent output, or internal confusion.
delegationNotes: A marketing manager can run the planning cadence once clear commercial priorities and approval rules exist, but founders should still set the top monthly themes.
automationNotes: Use shared planning tools, template briefs, and recurring meeting cadences to reduce planning friction and keep asset creation visible.
